summary: Iteration 3 regression — Employees/Attendance/CSV-import. 13/13 new backend tests + 15/15 existing backend tests pass (28/28 total). Mobile-web UI flow (login -> Staff tab -> attendance cycle -> add employee -> Expenses -> CSV import) all working. All new testIDs present and functional.

issues: Seed creates the same 8 employee names in every branch, so 'All Branches' view shows Ankit Patel × 3, Arjun Rao × 3 etc. Not a bug (each is a distinct employee_id/branch), but could confuse demo viewers — consider per-branch name variants in seed_employees.

Expenses subtitle uses inr() which rounds to 1 decimal in Lakhs (e.g. ₹12.2L). After a small CSV import (₹2k), the displayed total does not visibly change even though the new entries are added correctly — cosmetic rounding only; Recent Entries / backend values are correct.

action_items: (Optional) Diversify employee names across branches in seed_employees() so the 'All Branches' employee list has 24 unique names instead of 8 names repeated 3 times.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 988 lines — still growing. Splitting into routers/{auth,employees,expenses,inventory,dashboard,ai,imports}.py is becoming important.
employees.tsx cycleStatus calls api.post without optimistic UI; each tap triggers full GET /employees. Fine for 24 rows but inefficient at scale — consider local state update + background sync.
Attendance upsert does not validate `date` format; any string is accepted. Add ISO-date regex validation on AttendanceRequest.
CSVImportRequest.rows uses Dict[str, Any] with float() coercion but no try/except — malformed CSV amount like 'abc' would 500 instead of returning a clean 400.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all critical iter3 flows — attendance cycle, add employee, CSV import, tab nav)

seed_data_creation: Each pytest module creates one TEST_iter3_<uuid>@restaurant.com user; UI run created iter3ui_<uuid>@restaurant.com. Seed yields 3 branches + 24 employees (8×3) + 30 days attendance + 90 days sales.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Canonical pytest suites: /app/backend/tests/test_restaurant_api.py (iter1-2) and /app/backend/tests/test_iter3_employees_csv.py (iter3). BASE_URL from EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL. Attendance cycle order (frontend): unmarked→present→half→leave→absent. CSV import types supported: 'sales' and 'expenses'. Staff tab is 4th position in bottom nav (7 tabs total). The expenses subtitle uses Lakh-rounded inr(); validate CSV imports via Recent Entries list or backend, not the header total.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no functional bugs identified. UI assertion for expenses total change was a false negative caused by Lakh-rounding in inr() formatter; backend CSV import test confirms inserted rows persist and the /api/expenses total increased by the correct amount.
